articles.title: Euro sticks to positive bias above 1.1400 vs USD; Mideast tensions cap gains
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair attracts some buyers for the second straight day, though it lacks follow-through and remains confined within the previous day's range during the Asian session on Thursday.

articles.title: Australian Dollar steadies following Chinese CPI data
articles.expand: AUD/USD gains ground after remaining flat in the previous day, trading around 0.6930 during the Asian hours on Thursday.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar holds ground despite softer China’s CPI data
articles.expand: NZD/USD extends its gains for the second successive day, trading around 0.5720 during the Asian hours on Thursday. The pair holds gains as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) remains stronger following the Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation data release from New Zealand’s close trading partner, China.

articles.title: FOMC Minutes Show Some Officials Were Ready to Hike in June
articles.expand: The minutes of the Federal Reserve’s June 16-17 meeting revealed that the unanimous decision to leave interest rates unchanged masked a noticeably more hawkish debate beneath the surface. While all policymakers ultimately supported keeping the federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75%, “a few participants commented that… there was a case for raising the target range” […] The post FOMC Minutes Show Some Officials Were Ready to Hike in June appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: British Pound strengthens to near 1.3400 as UK political risk fades
articles.expand: The GBP/USD pair gathers strength near 1.3395 during the Asian trading hours on Thursday, bolstered by fading domestic political uncertainty.

articles.title: Cotton Fades Back Lower on Wednesday
articles.expand: Cotton futures saw modest weakness on a Wednesday correction, with contracts 6 to 73 points lower. The outside markets were generally stronger, as Crude oil was up $4.32 following the US striking Iran overnight and again today, as President Trump stated the ceasefire is over. The US also revoked waivers...

articles.title: Japanese Yen gains ground on intervention risks
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair edges lower to around 162.45 during the early Asian session on Thursday. The Japanese Yen (JPY) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) amid fears of possible intervention from Japanese authorities. The US weekly Initial Jobless Claims report is due later on Thursday.

articles.title: Gold declines below $4,100 as US–Iran tensions revive inflation worries, Fed rate hike bets
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) declines to around $4,075 during the early Asian session on Thursday. The precious metal extends its downside as US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran has ended, stoking concerns that a renewal of war could again drive inflation and push up interest rates.

articles.title: Euro steadies as Middle East strikes offsets Fed repricing
articles.expand: The Euro holds firm against the US Dollar on Wednesday as hostilities in the Middle East continued, with the US attacking Iran for the second straight day at around the Strait of Hormuz. At the time of writing, the EUR/USD trades at 1.1420, after bouncing off daily lows of 1.1391.

articles.title: TA Alert of the Day: GBP/JPY Keltner Channel Breach Flags Potential Pullback
articles.expand: GBP/JPY’s upper Keltner Channel breach puts focus on whether buyers can sustain momentum or whether price snaps back inside the channel.

articles.title: The Australian Dollar flatlines while everyone else has a war to trade
articles.expand: AUD/USD trades pinned between 0.6900 and 0.6950 on Wednesday, essentially unchanged and printing the kind of indecision candle that tells traders the week's rebound has run out of sponsorship.

articles.title: British Pound Sterling wins the day and stays stuck in the same trap
articles.expand: GBP/USD trades just below 1.3400 on Wednesday, up around a quarter of a percent and once again leaning on the 200-day Exponential Moving Average (EMA) that has repelled every advance since the pair clawed back from its mid-June washout.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Market probes intervention risk – ABN AMRO
articles.expand: Georgette Boele at ABN AMRO highlights persistent weakness in the Japanese Yen as investors test authorities’ tolerance for depreciation. Fiscal expansion and a dovish Bank of Japan stance weigh on the currency, while Japan’s energy-importer status adds pressure.

articles.title: Financial & Forex Market Recap – July 8, 2026
articles.expand: Trump called the U.S.-Iran ceasefire “over” after fresh strikes, sending oil higher and risk assets lower. Fed minutes lifted hike bets and yields, but the dollar still slipped.

articles.title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: False breakout at 0.8100 triggers pullback
articles.expand: The USD/CHF pair recoils after reaching a five-day high of 0.8108 on Wednesday, edging down some 0.02% as risk appetite deteriorates due to US President Donald Trump’s suggestion of an end to the ceasefire, as Iran attacked ships on Tuesday.

articles.title: Forex Today: US Dollar struggles despite hawkish FOMC Minutes
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) trades near the 101.00 region on Wednesday as investors digest the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) Minutes from the June 16-17 meeting, the first under Federal Reserve (Fed) Chair Kevin Warsh.

articles.title: Thai Baht: Range-bound after inflation data against dollar – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Charlie Lay highlights that USD/THB edged higher as Thai inflation data support a steady policy stance. Lay links the move to expectations that the Bank of Thailand (BoT) will keep interest rates on hold at 1% through at least the next meeting.

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Bears retain control within descending channel
articles.expand: EUR/USD holds firm on Wednesday after reversing earlier losses triggered by renewed tensions between the United States (US) and Iran, while traders digest the June Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting minutes.

articles.title: Fed Minutes Confirm Mixed Views About Outlook For Rates
articles.expand: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released the minutes of its June 16-17 monetary policy meeting, which marked Kevin Warsh's first meeting as head of the central bank. Amid considerable uncertainty surrounding the outlook for inflation, the minutes said participants discussed a range of scenarios for the evolution of the economy and for future monetary policy actions.

articles.title: US Dollar Index shrugs at unanimous Fed hold that reads anything but unanimous
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) spent Wednesday travelling in circles, grinding around 101.00 after the Minutes of the June 16-17 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting crossed the wires at 18:00 GMT, capping a session in which traders had already faded another round of Middle East friction.

articles.title: Breaking: FOMC Minutes lean toward higher-for-longer narrative
articles.expand: The June FOMC Minutes reinforced the Fed’s cautious approach as policymakers unanimously agreed to maintain interest rates unchanged while keeping a close eye on inflation risks.

articles.title: New Zealand Dollar: RBNZ tightening supports upside – ABN AMRO
articles.expand: ABN AMRO’s Georgette Boele reports that the Reserve Bank of New Zealand lifted its policy rate by 25 bp to 2.5% and signalled more tightening is likely. Despite some easing in energy prices, the bank sees persistent inflation pressures and scope for further reduction in stimulus.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Bears now look at the 200-day SMA
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar remains unable to find sustainable upside traction, keeping its trade below the key 0.7000 yardstick for now. So far, the constructive tone should remain unchallenged while above its critical 200-day SMA.

articles.title: 2.50%: Why the Kiwi's first hike in three years is a wager on a number nobody can see
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) raised the Official Cash Rate (OCR) by 25 basis points to 2.50% at 02:00 GMT on Wednesday, its first hike in three years and the moment the bank that cut deeper than any G10 peer last cycle turned to face the other way.

articles.title: RBNZ Raises OCR to 2.50%
articles.expand: The RBNZ raised its Official Cash Rate to 2.50%, its first hike in three years, but won’t commit to a timeline for what comes next.

articles.title: Pound Awaits Tighter Policy from Bank of England
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex GBP/USD declined to 1.3352 on Wednesday amid a general deterioration in the external environment and a decline in risk appetite. The escalation of tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and Iran’s attacks on facilities in Kuwait and Bahrain have prompted investors to move away from riskier assets. Earlier, the pound had […]

articles.title: Oil Tests a Return of War Premium as Markets Await the Fed’s Hidden Debate
articles.expand: Markets were driven by two competing sources of uncertainty today as Brent crude surged toward the $80 mark while investors awaited the release of the June FOMC minutes for the first real insight into Chair Kevin Warsh’s policy thinking. Dollar traded firmer alongside oil, while Kiwi outperformed following the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s rate […] The post Oil Tests a Return of War Premium as Markets Await the Fed’s Hidden Debate appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Australian Dollar After RBA's Hunter Hawkish Remarks
articles.expand: The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday, as the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) kept the door open for additional monetary policy tightening, if necessary, to bring inflation back to the central bank's target.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Rises Against Majors
articles.expand: The Australian dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.title: New Zealand Central Bank Hikes Rate For First Time Since 2023
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand raised its benchmark interest rate for the first time in more than three years to bring inflation to target and hinted at further tightening. The Monetary Policy Committee, governed by Anna Breman, decided to lift the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50 percent. This was the first hike since May 2023.

articles.title: NZ Dollar Rises Against Majors
articles.expand: The New Zealand dollar strengthened against other major currencies in the Asian session on Wednesday.

articles.title: NZD/USD Recovers After RBNZ, but Higher Bar for Next Hike Caps Rally
articles.expand: The New Zealand dollar received the boost that normally accompanies a rate hike, but the rally quickly lost momentum. The Reserve Bank of New Zealand delivered a widely anticipated 25 basis point increase in the Official Cash Rate to 2.50%, yet investors stopped short of pricing a more aggressive tightening cycle. Instead, the market came […] The post NZD/USD Recovers After RBNZ, but Higher Bar for Next Hike Caps Rally appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: RBNZ Raises OCR, Consensus Masks Diverging Inflation Views
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand raised the Official Cash Rate by 25 basis points to 2.50%, with the Monetary Policy Committee reaching the decision by consensus despite differing assessments of the inflation outlook. Policymakers agreed that a further withdrawal of monetary stimulus was warranted to return inflation to the 2% midpoint, even though the […] The post RBNZ Raises OCR, Consensus Masks Diverging Inflation Views appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: RBA’s Hunter: Supply Shocks Don’t Mean Inflation Can Be Ignored
articles.expand: Reserve Bank of Australia Assistant Governor Sarah Hunter said central banks cannot automatically dismiss inflation caused by supply shocks, arguing that persistent disruptions may still require a monetary policy response. “While supply shocks create difficult trade-offs, they do not lessen the importance of maintaining low and stable inflation,” she said, emphasizing that the RBA remains […] The post RBA’s Hunter: Supply Shocks Don’t Mean Inflation Can Be Ignored appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: BoJ’s Asada: Demand-Driven Inflation Needed Before Another Rate Hike
articles.expand: Bank of Japan board member Toichiro Asada said he would only support another interest rate hike once inflation is clearly being driven by stronger domestic demand rather than temporary cost pressures. Speaking after dissenting against the BoJ’s June rate increase, Asada said, “I would support another rate hike only after confirming demand-driven inflation,” signaling that […] The post BoJ’s Asada: Demand-Driven Inflation Needed Before Another Rate Hike appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Chart Art: AUD/JPY Testing Support Turned Resistance Zone
articles.expand: AUD/JPY recently fell through the floor around the 112.50 minor psychological mark and has since pulled up for a retest. Will the area of interest hold as a ceiling this time?

articles.title: Speech: Understanding Supply Shocks and Their Implications for Monetary Policy
articles.expand: Speech by Sarah Hunter, Assistant Governor (Economic), to the Australian Conference of Economists 2026

articles.title: Yen Still Under Pressure: Markets Await Action from Authorities
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex USD/JPY is holding near 161.84 on Tuesday, with the yen close to 40-year lows. Pressure on the Japanese currency remains as market participants continue to bet against it, with no visible currency interventions from Japanese authorities having materialised. At the same time, investors are closely monitoring potential action from Tokyo. Japanese […]

articles.title: The Next Dollar Catalyst Is CPI. The Bigger Story Is Inside the Fed.
articles.expand: Financial markets spent much of today’s session waiting rather than reacting. Most major currency pairs traded comfortably within yesterday’s ranges as investors resisted making large directional bets ahead of tomorrow’s RBNZ decision and next week’s US inflation report. Dollar and Yen were the day’s strongest performers, but neither attracted enough follow-through buying to establish fresh […] The post The Next Dollar Catalyst Is CPI. The Bigger Story Is Inside the Fed. appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: ESRB warns of vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models
articles.expand: The European Systemic Risk Board (ESRB), the EU’s macroprudential oversight authority, is issuing a formal warning regarding vulnerabilities in the financial system linked to frontier AI models. The ESRB's warning calls for relevant authorities to reflect these risks in supervisory and oversight work.

articles.title: ECB’s Panetta: Fragile Outlook Means No Predetermined Path for Rates
articles.expand: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta said the Eurozone economy continues to face a delicate balance between persistent inflation risks and weakening growth, arguing that policymakers should avoid committing to any predetermined path for interest rates. “The outlook remains fragile. Upside risks to inflation continue to coexist with downside risks to growth,” he […] The post ECB’s Panetta: Fragile Outlook Means No Predetermined Path for Rates appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: GBP/CHF Nears Trend-Reversal Zone as Political Risk Fades and Sterling Shorts Unwind
articles.expand: Sterling’s rally has been about more than better sentiment toward the UK. It has been driven by the disappearance of one of the market’s biggest bearish trades. As political uncertainty faded following the resolution of Labour’s leadership transition, investors who had built sizeable short Sterling positions found themselves on the wrong side of the market. […] The post GBP/CHF Nears Trend-Reversal Zone as Political Risk Fades and Sterling Shorts Unwind appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: ECB’s Schnabel: Falling Oil Prices Don’t Mean Inflation Battle Is Over
articles.expand: European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel cautioned that the recent decline in oil prices should not be interpreted as a return to pre-war inflation conditions, arguing that underlying price pressures remain elevated. “Does the decline in oil prices mean that we are back to the pre-war situation? I don’t think so,” she said […] The post ECB’s Schnabel: Falling Oil Prices Don’t Mean Inflation Battle Is Over appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Has the recovery kicked in?
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar remains in a consolidative phase below the key 0.7000 threshold for now, while the constructive tone remains unchanged above the critical 200-day SMA.

articles.title: 2 Reasons to Sell the Australia Dollar Here
articles.expand: September Australian dollar futures present a selling opportunity on more price weakness.

articles.title: Oil prices have stabilized. The Canadian dollar continues to trade near yearly lows.

The S&P 500 Index (US500) gained 2.33%. The Technology‑heavy NASDAQ Index (US100) closed higher by 1.00% over five trading days. This week, market […]

articles.title: EUR/USD in a Narrow Range: Focus on Fed Minutes
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex EUR/USD is trading around 1.1432 on Monday. At the end of last week, the main currency pair posted modest gains. Weaker-than-expected US labour market data and lower oil prices have weighed on the US dollar, prompting investors to reconsider expectations for further Federal Reserve policy tightening. The Non-Farm Payrolls report released […]

articles.title: USD/JPY Bulls Return as Japan’s Intervention Window Closes
articles.expand: USD/JPY bulls are back. After last week’s intervention scare briefly knocked the pair lower, Yen selling returned today as the most obvious window for Japanese action passed without official intervention. Dollar’s rebound also helped, with markets now appearing to have fully absorbed the dovish surprise from June non-farm payrolls. But the sharper story is in […] The post USD/JPY Bulls Return as Japan’s Intervention Window Closes appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Eurozone Producer Inflation Holds Firm as Core Factory Prices Continue to Rise
articles.expand: Eurozone PPI rose 0.2% mom in May, matching market expectations, while annual producer inflation accelerated from 5.0% yoy to 5.9% yoy, slightly above the 5.7% yoy consensus. The data suggest producer price pressures remained elevated even as monthly price growth moderated from April’s 0.7% increase. The monthly increase reflected diverging trends across sectors. Prices for […] The post Eurozone Producer Inflation Holds Firm as Core Factory Prices Continue to Rise appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Eurozone Retail Sales Rise 0.2% mom in May as Food Spending Leads Growth
articles.expand: Eurozone retail sales rose 0.2% mom in May, matching market expectations and pointing to continued resilience in consumer spending despite a mixed performance across retail categories. Across the broader EU, retail sales increased a stronger 0.5% mom, suggesting consumer demand remained relatively firm in several member states. The monthly increase in the Eurozone was driven […] The post Eurozone Retail Sales Rise 0.2% mom in May as Food Spending Leads Growth appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: Eurozone Sentix Investor Confidence Surges to -3.1 as Germany’s Recovery Gains Traction
articles.expand: Eurozone investor confidence improved sharply in July, with the Sentix Investor Confidence Index rising from -13.4 to -3.1, marking a third consecutive monthly gain. The improvement was broad-based, with the Current Situation Index climbing from -20.0 to -14.8, while the Expectations Index jumped from -6.5 to 9.3, returning to positive territory as optimism over the […] The post Eurozone Sentix Investor Confidence Surges to -3.1 as Germany’s Recovery Gains Traction appeared first on ActionForex .

articles.title: The US Dollar just beat the Swiss Franc at its own safe-haven game
articles.expand: As the king among safe havens, the Swiss Franc (CHF) is supposed to benefit from geopolitical shocks such as the Iran war. This time, it didn’t.
